text.title: Predictive Analytics for PM Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Predictive Analytics for PM. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Training corpus MUST have ≥30 completed projects with structured outcome labels. Smaller corpora overfit.
rule.rationale: ML models trained on <30 samples produce poorly-calibrated forecasts; the minimum is empirically calibrated.

rule.statement: Forecasts MUST emit probability intervals (80% PI + 95% PI), not point estimates. Point estimates forbidden.
rule.rationale: Point estimates miscommunicate certainty; probability intervals expose model uncertainty honestly.

rule.statement: Calibration (predicted PI coverage vs actual coverage) MUST be checked quarterly on held-out data. Stale calibration forbidden.
rule.rationale: Calibration drifts with portfolio composition; quarterly check catches degradation before it harms decisions.

rule.statement: Feature spec MUST be versioned; rebuild on feature change. Mixing features across versions in one model run is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed-version features cause silent train/serve skew; version pinning prevents the bug.

rule.statement: Each forecast MUST include top-3 contributing features with shap/lime-style attribution. Black-box outputs forbidden.
rule.rationale: Black-box forecasts cannot inform action; attributed top features give PMO levers for corrective work.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Predictive Analytics for PM fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Predictive Analytics for PM outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
